Empires & Puzzles Cupcake Review: The 4-Star Minion Thief

Don't let the cute face fool you. Cupcake is a 'Teenage Gefjon' who steals minions and hits hard. But with aging stats, is this 4-star Rogue still worth your feeders in 2026? We analyze the math behind the meow.
Cupcake Review: Is This Kitty Still The Purr-fect Minion Thief?
Cupcake, The Clawsome Bandit: Minion Thief & Teenage Gefjon

Cupcake is a 4-star Epic hero from the Mighty Pets family, originally released in late 2022. In a game full of terrifying dragons and cosmic horrors, Cupcake is a refreshing reminder that sometimes the most dangerous things come in small, adorable packages.

Often affectionately dubbed "Teenage Gefjon" or "Baby Gefjon" by the community, Cupcake brings a mechanic typically reserved for Legendary heroes—Minion Stealing—down to the Epic level. While she lacks the raw stat power of the latest 2026 releases, her utility remains unique in the 4-star arena.

Cupcake’s design is a delightful blend of "cute" and "lethal." She’s a black cat rogue clad in purple assassin gear, dual-wielding curved blades (or perhaps modified claws?) and a whip. The backdrop suggests a rustic farm setting, perhaps hinting at her humble origins before she turned to a life of organized crime... or at least organized minion theft. The "determined kitty" expression is perfect—she's not here to play with yarn; she's here to steal your meat shields.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Mighty Pets family adds significant survivability and punishing mechanics against specific metas.
The family bonus buffs the stats of minions she steals or summons (from other sources) and nerfs enemy fiends. It's a nice cherry on top, especially if you pair her with other pets like
This is excellent. If you run her with a minion summoner (like a Christmas hero or a modern Summoner), she gains passive sustainability. 5% HP per turn adds up.
If an enemy summons a fiend (e.g.,
or newer Fiend makers), she automatically slaps all enemies for 85% damage. It's free chip damage that punishes the enemy for doing what they want to do.

Upon release, Cupcake was hailed as a "Mini-Gefjon." At the time, the minion meta (M.N., Christmas family, etc.) was rampant, and having a 4-star who could dismantle those walls was invaluable for Epic tournaments.
been power crept; it remains the gold standard for anti-minion snipers. However, her stats have been crept. A 400% hit off 690 Attack is less scary when enemies have 1000+ Defense.
She is no longer a general-purpose sniper. You don't bring her just for damage. You bring her specifically when:
Minions count as buffs, and in Bloody Battle, stealing minions is one of the few ways to "heal" (gaining HP via shields).
When facing a weakened team with a annoying minion summoner left alive.
She shines here, turning the war rule against the enemy.

In a fast-paced meta, 10 tiles can feel like an eternity for a sniper.
Her base stats are low compared to 2025/2026 heroes.
She offers no board control or AoE damage.
Stealing minions gives her buffs (if she steals multiple) and denies the enemy theirs.
Minions are "fake healing." Stealing them is one of the best sustain strategies in this mode.

Pair her with a fast minion summoner (like a 3-turn CD hero or a fast Xmas elf). Every time they summon, Cupcake gets 5% HP and Mana.
These heroes destroy minions. If Cupcake steals minions, she becomes vulnerable to minion-destroying heroes (though usually, you bring her
Some newer heroes punish you for having minions. Be careful stealing them if the enemy has a "Damage to target with Minions" hero.

Cupcake is a fantastic pull. She solves a specific problem (Minions) that often walls new players in wars and raids. Level her up, but don't feel pressured to Limit Break her immediately unless you love 4-star tournaments.
She is a luxury tool. You likely have 5-star heroes who do this (Gefjon, Grimble, etc.). However, for the
, she is arguably best-in-slot against heavy minion defenses. She is worth keeping but maybe not worth Alpha Aethers unless you are competitive in tournaments.
The community reception to Cupcake has always been warm, mostly due to her art and specific utility.
— This sentiment was echoed widely upon release.
"Why isn’t cupcake fast! Because he will make Gefjon look like crap."
— A user noted the delicate balance SG had to strike. If she were Fast, she would arguably be better than her 5-star counterpart in many scenarios.
"Stealing minions from 1 target for average speed is bad... Gormek costume is much better."
— Some players prefer the mass-destruction of C.Gormek, which is a fair point. Cupcake is for surgical removal; Gormek is for bulldozing.

Cupcake is the definition of a "Specialist." She isn't the hero you put on your defense team (usually), and she isn't the first hero you reach for in a generic raid. But when you see a team relying on a single, annoying minion tank, or a tournament board full of summoners, Cupcake is the skeleton key that unlocks the win.
She might be getting older, and her claws might not be as sharp as the new plasma-swords of 2026, but she knows how to steal a win—literally.
Keep one for tournaments. Feed duplicates unless you really, really hate minions.
A comprehensive review of Cupcake, the 4-star Dark hero from the Mighty Pets family. Analyze her stats, minion-stealing skill, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
